basics.name: Javier Loria
basics.label: Data Architect · Semantic Models on the Microsoft Data Platform
basics.summary: Data architect with 35+ years in software and 25+ specializing in business intelligence and semantic models on the Microsoft data platform. Founder and mentor at Primus Data. I focus on hands-on knowledge transfer — learning by doing — so every client builds lasting internal capability.

work.name: Primus Data
work.summary: Founder of a data consultancy specializing in the Microsoft data platform, with a team certified in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.
highlights: Design, build, and rollout of corporate data warehouses, data lakehouses, data marts, and semantic models on Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and SQL Server.
"Learning by doing" mentoring for client teams including Dole, El Colono, and Motiva.
Coordinated teams of up to 40 Fabric-certified professionals for support, SQL optimization, and continuous improvement.

work.name: Solid Quality Mentors
work.summary: Data warehouse and BI mentoring and consulting on the Microsoft data platform.
highlights: DW/BI mentoring and consulting for clients such as Capital One, Atlanta Gas Light Company, Microsoft Spain, and Dole/Standard Fruit Co.
Author and co-author of official Microsoft courses (MS 2781, MS 2782A, MS 2784A) and SQL Server training materials.

work.name: New Horizons Costa Rica
work.summary: General management of the IT training center.

work.name: New Horizons Costa Rica
highlights: Taught advanced Microsoft and Novell courses: Windows NT, Visual C# and Visual Basic, SQL Server (7.0 onward), SSAS, MDX, and BizTalk.

work.name: Micromundo
highlights: Designed and implemented POS systems and custom C++ solutions for hospitality and retail chains.

awards.title: Microsoft MVP — Visual Studio
awards.summary: Most Valuable Professional award recognizing community and technical leadership (2005).

awards.title: Microsoft MVP — SQL Server
awards.summary: Most Valuable Professional award recognizing community and technical leadership (2001–2002).
